methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rapidus, a late-stage semiconductor company founded by Atsuyoshi Koike, has raised $5.0B+ to date. The relevant peer universe spans 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private competitors including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method proved applicable. This approach applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $5.0B total capital raised, treating it as a valuation proxy at late stage, yielding $6.0B. Peer-Median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow semiconductor subsector—14 same-sector peers exist but none met the threshold of three filtered datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed because Rapidus's revenue is not on file; while the public semiconductor universe shows a median P/S of 20.6x across the three tickers, applying this multiple requires disclosed revenue data absent for private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median estimate of $6.0B. This range reflects reliance on a single triangulation method, creating meaningful uncertainty. Key sensitivities include actual revenue generation relative to sector multiples and the magnitude of late-stage to pre-exit step-up. Additional revenue or profitability disclosure would materially improve precision.
